item.title: Vosarogo urges Mataqali Nabukarabe patience over Nasarava payment dispute, assures payment will be done
fijivillage Straight Talk live at 7pm Wednesday on HIV cases and the work underway
item.description: A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, will be held live at 7pm Wednesday from Fiji Showcase 2026 at the Vodafone Arena. The guests on the show will be led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.
item.content:encoded:
A special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan focusing on HIV and the work of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, will be held live at 7pm Wednesday from Fiji Showcase 2026 at the Vodafone Arena.

The guests on the show will be led by the Chair of the National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Taskforce, Doctor Jason Mitchell.

We have a town-hall set up for this live show where you can also be part of the audience to participate on the show, and ask questions that you will have to send in by 8am next Tuesday.

You can send your questions focusing on the HIV statistics in Fiji, where we are at in terms of community-led health outreach, and the clinical prevention rollouts underway.

You can either send in your questions to vijaynarayan@fm96.com.fj or refer to our fijivillage social media pages to put in your questions.

If your question is selected to be asked on the show, you have to be a Fiji Showcase 2026 patron on Wednesday evening, to ask the question in person.

You can watch the show on our facebook page, YouTube channel, TikTok and X account, fijivillage at 7pm Wednesday.

You can also catch the podcast, fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.

item.title: Vani Catanasiga to appear on fijivillage Her-Story at 7pm Tuesday
item.description: Behind the title is a woman with a powerful story to tell. This Tuesday at 7pm, Fiji Council of Social Services Executive Director Vani Catanasiga joins Her-Story with Temalesi Vono to speak about life as a divorcee and mother of two, the pressures of her role, the loss of her father, the challenges she faces, and her vision for the organisation. Watch live on our Facebook page, YouTube, TikTok an
item.content:encoded: Behind the title is a woman with a powerful story to tell. This Tuesday at 7pm, Fiji Council of Social Services Executive Director Vani Catanasiga joins Her-Story with Temalesi Vono to speak about life as a divorcee and mother of two, the pressures of her role, the loss of her father, the challenges she faces, and her vision for the organisation. Watch live on our Facebook page, YouTube, TikTok and X account - fijivillage

item.title: Bernard opposes minimum wage increase until wage system gaps are fixed
FTUC warns against reducing workers’ FNPF contribution rates
item.description: The Fiji Trades Union Congress is calling on the Government to fulfil its promise of restoring the Fiji National Provident Fund to a tripartite board structure, saying workers and employers should once again have equal representation alongside Government. Speaking on the FTUC's budget submission ahead of Friday's 2026/2027 National Budget announcement, National Secretary Felix Anthony says the coa
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Trades Union Congress is calling on the Government to fulfil its promise of restoring the Fiji National Provident Fund to a tripartite board structure, saying workers and employers should once again have equal representation alongside Government.

Speaking on the FTUC's budget submission ahead of Friday's 2026/2027 National Budget announcement, National Secretary Felix Anthony says the coalition government had pledged to reinstate the tripartite governance model but is yet to deliver on that commitment.

Anthony says the union expected the change to be implemented during the Government's first year in office and hopes it will finally be addressed.

He also voiced strong opposition to any proposal to reduce FNPF contribution rates, warning that such a move would further weaken workers' retirement savings.

Anthony says workers had already sacrificed millions of dollars during the COVID-19 pandemic through reduced contributions and withdrawals from their accounts.

He stresses that lower contribution rates would not provide meaningful assistance to workers and would instead leave them with less money for retirement.

According to Anthony, around 86 percent of FNPF members have balances of less than $40,000, while 76 percent have savings below $20,000.

He says these figures highlight the need for a review of the FNPF pension scheme to ensure retirees receive adequate and sustainable pension payments.

Anthony also called on the Fund to address concerns raised by pensioners who were negatively affected by pension reforms introduced under the previous government.

The FTUC is also seeking a dedicated trade union grant to support worker education programmes on employment rights, occupational health and safety, and labour laws.

Anthony says such support will be especially important as Fiji prepares for anticipated labour law reforms.

He further urged the Government to reaffirm its commitment to the labour law review process, noting that legislation tabled in Parliament more than a year ago has yet to be enacted.

While many of the issues raised by the FTUC have been discussed with Government over several years, Anthony says progress has been slow.

He notes that commitments relating to worker education grants, wages council reviews and the restoration of the FNPF tripartite board structure have been made before but are yet to be fully implemented.

The 2026/2027 National Budget will be delivered at 10am Friday.
